allVerifiers.description: All verifiers that were ever routed to by this gateway.

entries.name: MainnetInbox
entries.description: The core Layer 1 entrypoint for the Taiko rollup where L2 block batches are proposed and their corresponding state transitions are proven. Forced inclusion submission and processing are disabled in this implementation. Proposals must come from the configured proposer checker, and if the configured prover whitelist is non-empty, proofs from non-whitelisted provers revert; the configured permissionless proving delay is not used to open proving.

noForce.description: Forced inclusions are disabled in this implementation: saveForcedInclusion() always reverts and propose() only accepts zero forced inclusions.

entries.name: MainnetVerifier
entries.description: Immutable verifier policy contract for Taiko mainnet. Each accepted proof must contain exactly two ordered sub-proofs: either SGX-GETH plus SGX-RETH/RISC0-RETH/SP1-RETH, or SGX-RETH plus SGX-GETH/RISC0-RETH/SP1-RETH. It routes each sub-proof to the corresponding downstream verifier.

sgxGethVerifier.description: SGX verifier for Geth-based proofs. Each accepted proof must include either this verifier or the SGX-RETH verifier.

sgxRethVerifier.description: SGX verifier for Reth-based proofs. Each accepted proof must include either this verifier or the SGX-GETH verifier.

risc0RethVerifier.description: RISC Zero verifier for Reth-based proofs that can serve as the second required verifier.

sp1RethVerifier.description: SP1 verifier for Reth-based proofs that can serve as the second required verifier.

opVerifier.description: Optional optimistic verifier slot. It is zero in the mainnet verifier constructor and cannot satisfy the policy.

tdxGethVerifier.description: Optional TDX verifier slot. It is zero in the mainnet verifier constructor and cannot satisfy the policy.

entries.name: TaikoSP1Verifier
entries.description: Gating router contract to verify batches using SP1.

trustedPrograms.description: Taiko-specific SP1 program verification keys trusted by this verifier gateway. Only proofs for these programs can be successfully verified.

verifier.description: Currently used Risc0 verifier contract, selector determined manually from calldata

entries.name: DefaultResolver
entries.description: Maps chainId-name pairs to contract addresses. Bridge and vault contracts resolve their counterparties through this registry, so changes in this mapping effectively act as contract upgrades. The pause function is intentionally disabled in this implementation.

entries.name: OptimisticTokenVotingPlugin
entries.description: An optimistic governance module. Standard proposals pass and can be executed unless 10% of votable TAIKO veto them within 7d. Emergency proposals can be executed without delay.

minVetoRatio.description: this value /1000000 is the threshold ratio (relative to voting supply) to veto a proposal.

governanceSettings.description: Governance parameters controlling veto threshold, minimum voting duration, post-vote timelock, L2 inactivity timeout, L2 veto aggregation grace period, and whether L2 voting power is ignored.

entries.name: MainnetERC20Vault
entries.description: Shared vault for Taiko chains for bridged ERC20 tokens. Pausing stops token sends, message-triggered releases, and recalls. Released or minted tokens are subject to the configured quota manager.

receivedPermissions.description: consume ERC20 withdrawal quota when tokens leave the vault.

quotaManager.description: Quota manager that rate-limits ERC20 tokens released or minted by this vault.

btokenDenylist.description: Bridged tokens denied by migration logic. Denied tokens cannot be sent through the vault.

receivedPermissions.description: register SGX instances after DCAP attestation verification.
register SGX instances after DCAP attestation verification.
pause and unpause signal proof verification.
update token withdrawal quotas and the quota refill period.
pause and unpause bridge message flows and fund the bridge through direct ETH transfers.
manage the prover whitelist.
manage the ejecter role.

entries.name: DAO
entries.description: The main contract and entrypoint of the Aragon-based DAO governance framework. Fine-grained DAO permissions, proposals, voting and thresholds are configured here.

permsGranted.description: Granted permissions in the DAO. Need to be manually compared to the revoked ones and hardcoded permissions need to be adjusted on change. Use AI with L1 contract context.

permsRevoked.description: Revoked permissions in the DAO. Need to be manually compared to the granted ones.

entries.name: SignalService
entries.description: Facilitates secure cross-chain message passing by storing signals and state-root checkpoints. Bridge escrows and other applications use it to prove that a specific L1<->L2 signal or checkpointed state transition occurred via Merkle proofs. Pausing disables signal proof verification.

entries.name: QuotaManager
entries.description: Defines withdrawal quotas for ETH and ERC20 releases from the shared bridge. A token quota of zero means unlimited withdrawals for that token.

bridge.description: Bridge contract allowed to consume ETH withdrawal quota.

erc20Vault.description: ERC20 vault allowed to consume token withdrawal quota.

quotaPeriod.description: Time in seconds for consumed quota to fully refill.

tokenQuotas.description: Configured withdrawal quotas per refill period. Missing or zero quotas are treated as unlimited.

entries.name: MainnetBridge
entries.description: Shared bridge escrow for Taiko chains for bridged ETH and arbitrary bridge messages. Pausing stops sending, processing, recalling, retrying, and failing messages. ETH released from the bridge is subject to the configured quota manager.

receivedPermissions.description: consume ETH withdrawal quota when ETH leaves the bridge.

quotaManager.description: Quota manager that rate-limits ETH released by processed or recalled bridge messages.

signalService.description: Signal service used to prove cross-chain message delivery and failure signals.

entries.name: ProverWhitelist
entries.description: Defines the prover whitelist queried by the inbox before accepting proofs. If the inbox is configured with this contract and the whitelist is non-empty, only whitelisted provers can prove proposals.

entries.name: RiscZeroGroth16Verifier
entries.description: Verifier contract for RISC Zero Groth16 proofs. This older implementation exposes control-root and selector constants but does not expose a VERSION getter.

entries.name: TaikoDAOController
entries.description: Middleware contract that maintains ownership of DAO-controlled assets and contracts. Its token weight does not count towards the DAO quorum.

entries.name: PreconfWhitelist
entries.description: Contains the whitelist of addresses eligible to propose batches on L1 and issue preconfirmations. It dynamically selects a single active operator for each epoch using a delayed Ethereum beacon block root as randomness. There is no fallback proposer path in this contract: non-selected operators cannot propose for the current epoch.
